PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Aug-19-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Peter W. Clerides appearing by CourtCall for Garry D. Hall (Petitioner).

The Court stated before it today is a Petition for Probate of Lost Will, that this matter was on calendar five weeks ago, that there was discussion regarding presumption of revocation, and that there was additional information submitted by Mr. Clerides, Mr. Hall, and Mr. Kenny Thomas. The Court stated that no one had appeared to object to the petition. There being no one present to object, the Court stated for the record that the lost will dated July 17, 2012 is admitted to probate and directed Mr. Clerides to submit a new proposed order. The Court inquired about bond. Mr. Clerides stated that the will states that bond is not required and noted that the Court would need to appoint a probate referee. Mr. Clerides briefly addressed bond again stating that a minimum bond would be required. The Court set bond at $20,000.00. Status on close of the estate is set for November 16, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court then took the Petition for Probate of Lost Will under submission. The clerk noted that the name of the probate referee would be provided by the probate examiner.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jul-07-2020 Continued To Aug-11-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Clerides appeared by telephone for Garry Hall (Petitioner).

The Court referred to Probate Code 6124 presumption of revocation, stated that it seems there are some factors giving rise to revocation, that the will was last in the Decedent's possession and that the Decedent was competent until death. The Court also stated that it does not know what Mr. Hall's relationship was with the Father and that there is a question about Counsel's relationship with the Decedent. In addition, the Court stated that it needs something to rebut revocation. The Petition for Probate of Lost Will is continued to August 11, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Any additional evidence is to be filed by August 4, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.
